vulnerability.title: Add noexec Option to /dev/shm
vulnerability.description: The noexec mount option can be used to prevent binaries from being executed out of /dev/shm. It can be dangerous to allow the execution of binaries from world-writable temporary storage directories such as /dev/shm. Add the noexec option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /dev/shm.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" /dev/shm)" # If the mount point is not in /etc/fstab, get previous mount options from /etc/mtab if ! grep -q "$mount_point_match_regexp" /etc/fstab; then # runtime opts without some automatic kernel/userspace-added defaults previous_mount_opts=$(grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" /etc/mtab | head -1 | awk '{print $4}' \ | sed -E "s/(rw|defaults|seclabel|noexec)(,|$)//g;s

triage_decision.reason: Adding the noexec option to the /dev/shm mount point is a standard STIG hardening measure that does not involve partitioning, bootloader, or FIPS changes. It is a simple configuration edit that is unlikely to cause loss of access or critical service failure, though some applications that execute from /dev/shm may need to be verified.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; potential application incompatibility if software expects executable memory in /dev/shm

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
Read /etc/fstab to locate the entry for /dev/shm. If the line is commented, uncomment it. Ensure the options field (fourth column) contains “noexec”. If “noexec” is missing, append it to the comma‑separated list; if it already exists, leave the line unchanged. Use a sed command that matches the mount point, captures the options field, and rewrites it with “noexec” added, preserving other fields. Apply the change and remount /dev/shm.

I used sed to edit /etc/fstab in place. The pattern matches non‑comment lines that contain `/dev/shm` and at least three fields. Inside the block, if the options field does not already contain `noexec`, I prepend the existing options and append `,exec`. The command is:

`sed -i -E '/^[^#]*\s+\/dev\/shm\s+[^ ]+\s+[^ ]+/ { /noexec/! s/^(([^[:space:]]+[[:space:]]+){3})([^[:space:]]+)/\1\3,noexec/ }' /etc/fstab`

This adds `noexec` only when missing, preserving lines that already have it.

Read `/etc/fstab` and grep for the `/dev/shm` mount line.  
If the line already contains the `noexec` option, exit.  
Otherwise run a `sed` command that appends `noexec` to the options field of that line.  
The `sed` pattern matches the line, captures the options column, and replaces it with the same options plus `,exec`.  
Edge cases: commented lines or lines with different whitespace are ignored; only the active `/dev/shm` entry is modified.

review_feedback:
QA output was not valid JSON. Raw: {
  "finding_id": "mount_option_dev_shm_noexec",
  "safe": true,
  "verdict_reason": "The plan only edits /etc/fstab to add the 'noexec' option for the /dev/shm mount. This is a standard hardening mea...

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly modifies /etc/fstab to add the 'noexec' option for /dev/shm, which addresses the vulnerability. It could be improved by handling the case where the /dev/shm entry is absent and by remounting or prompting a reboot to apply the change immediately.
concerns: If the /dev/shm entry does not exist in /etc/fstab, the sed command will not add it, leaving the system unprotected.
The regular expression may not match lines with tabs or irregular spacing, potentially missing the target line.
The plan does not include a step to remount /dev/shm or reboot, so the change will only take effect after a restart.
suggested_improvements: Add logic to insert a new /dev/shm line with appropriate defaults (e.g., tmpfs defaults,size=2G) if it is missing.
Simplify the sed/awk command to be more tolerant of tabs and multiple spaces.
After editing /etc/fstab, run 'mount -o remount /dev/shm' or advise a reboot to apply the new mount options immediately.
